action.reasoning: Observation: Current best PESQ=3.137 using MetricGAN+ with fixed loudness norm to -25 LUFS (val PESQ flat at 2.862 across strategies/targets -35 to -23; iterative 2-pass worse at 2.843). Mean noisy ~ -24 LUFS. test_enhance.py works but was truncated previously. Analysis: Flat PESQ suggests broad invariance but matching normalization target to statistics of clean val files (mean clean LUFS) may better align enhanced outputs to expected clean levels, potentially improving test PESQ/cbak/covl slightly without risk. Still no training (ConvTasNet would need careful chunked dataset+SI-SDR+auraloss setup, ~5min/epoch); postproc refinements are faster to iterate. Decision: Rewrite test_enhance.py cleanly (add mean_clean_loudness computation + PESQ eval for it vs -25 on 100 samples, pick/save best target), run it to update best_loudness.txt, then submit_eval on metricgan_checkpoint (predict.py already uses fixed strategy + best_loudness.txt).
